We inject the prior information of reflection placement and reflection appearance into foundation diffusion model. We also divide reflections into two types and adopt type-aware model design. To support training, we construct the first large-scale object reflection dataset DEROBA. Experiments demonstrate that our method generates reflections that are physically coherent and visually realistic, establishing a new benchmark for reflection generation.

AI.tldr: 本工作聚焦于图像合成中的反射生成任务，通过向扩散模型注入反射位置和外观先验信息，并采用类型感知模型设计，构建了首个大规模物体反射数据集DEROBA，实验结果显示该方法在反射生成任务上建立了新的基准。
AI.motivation: 图像合成中前景对象与背景的环境一致性（如阴影和反射）很重要，阴影生成已有广泛研究，但反射生成仍相对欠探索。
AI.method: 将反射位置和外观的先验信息注入基础扩散模型，并将反射分为两种类型，采用类型感知模型设计进行训练。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能够生成物理一致且视觉真实的反射。
AI.conclusion: 该方法生成了物理一致且视觉真实的反射，为反射生成任务设定了新的基准。

relevance_reason: This paper is about 2D image synthesis (reflection generation) using diffusion models, completely unrelated to the research profile's focus on 3D human motion generation. While both use diffusion models, the application domain is entirely different - this is about generating reflections in composite images, not about converting 2D video to 3D motion, temporal consistency in visual-to-motion translation, or any of the specified pain points. The paper addresses image synthesis, not human motion generation.

title: ViT-Explainer: An Interactive Walkthrough of the Vision Transformer Pipeline
summary: Transformer-based architectures have become the shared backbone of natural language processing and computer vision. However, understanding how these models operate remains challenging, particularly in vision settings, where images are processed as sequences of patch tokens. Existing interpretability tools often focus on isolated components or expert-oriented analysis, leaving a gap in guided, end-to-end understanding of the full inference pipeline. To bridge this gap, we present ViT-Explainer, a web-based interactive system that provides an integrated visualization of Vision Transformer inference, from patch tokenization to final classification. The system combines animated walkthroughs, patch-level attention overlays, and a vision-adapted Logit Lens within both guided and free exploration modes. A user study with six participants suggests that ViT-Explainer is easy to learn and use, helping users interpret and understand Vision Transformer behavior.

AI.tldr: ViT-Explainer是一个基于Web的交互式可视化系统，用于解释Vision Transformer的推理过程，从patch分词到最终分类。
AI.motivation: 现有解释工具通常关注孤立组件或面向专家的分析，缺乏对视觉Transformer完整推理流程的引导式端到端理解。
AI.method: 提出了ViT-Explainer系统，集成了动画演示、patch级注意力叠加和适用于视觉的Logit Lens，包含引导式和自由探索两种模式。
AI.result: 用户研究（6名参与者）表明ViT-Explainer易于学习和使用，能有效帮助用户解释和理解Vision Transformer的行为。
AI.conclusion: ViT-Explainer是一个有效的视觉Transformer交互解释工具，通过用户研究验证了其可用性和帮助用户理解ViT行为的能力。

relevance_reason: The paper is about ViT-Explainer, an educational tool for explaining Vision Transformer architecture and its inference process. This is completely irrelevant to the research field of Computer Graphics - 3D Human Motion Generation. The application domain is fundamentally different: this paper deals with explainable AI/ML for image classification, not video-to-3D-motion translation, not human motion generation, not any of the methods mentioned (VLM for quality supervision, video generation models like Wanxiang/Seedance, or 3D motion generation). The research profile requires converting 2D videos to 3D motion with temporal consistency and quality control - this paper doesn't address any of these pain points or methods.

title: UniDriveVLA: Unifying Understanding, Perception, and Action Planning for Autonomous Driving
summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models have recently emerged in autonomous driving, with the promise of leveraging rich world knowledge to improve the cognitive capabilities of driving systems. However, adapting such models for driving tasks currently faces a critical dilemma between spatial perception and semantic reasoning. Consequently, existing VLA systems are forced into suboptimal compromises: directly adopting 2D Vision-Language Models yields limited spatial perception, whereas enhancing them with 3D spatial representations often impairs the native reasoning capacity of VLMs. We argue that this dilemma largely stems from the coupled optimization of spatial perception and semantic reasoning within shared model parameters. To overcome this, we propose UniDriveVLA, a Unified Driving Vision-Language-Action model based on Mixture-of-Transformers that addresses the perception-reasoning conflict via expert decoupling. Specifically, it comprises three experts for driving understanding, scene perception, and action planning, which are coordinated through masked joint attention. In addition, we combine a sparse perception paradigm with a three-stage progressive training strategy to improve spatial perception while maintaining semantic reasoning capability. Extensive experiments show that UniDriveVLA achieves state-of-the-art performance in open-loop evaluation on nuScenes and closed-loop evaluation on Bench2Drive. Moreover, it demonstrates strong performance across a broad range of perception, prediction, and understanding tasks, including 3D detection, online mapping, motion forecasting, and driving-oriented VQA, highlighting its broad applicability as a unified model for autonomous driving. Code and model have been released at https://github.com/xiaomi-research/unidrivevla

AI.tldr: 提出UniDriveVLA，一个基于Mixture-of-Transformers的统一驾驶视觉语言动作模型，通过专家解耦解决感知与推理冲突，在nuScenes开环和Bench2闭环评估中实现最优性能。
AI.motivation: 现有VLA模型在驾驶任务中面临空间感知与语义推理之间的关键困境：直接采用2D VLM空间感知能力有限，而增强3D空间表示往往会损害VLMs的原生推理能力。这种困境源于空间感知和语义推理在共享模型参数中的耦合优化。
AI.method: 提出基于Mixture-of-Transformers的UniDriveVLA模型，通过专家解耦解决感知-推理冲突。具体包含三个专家：驾驶理解、场景感知和动作规划，通过掩码联合注意力协调。结合稀疏感知范式和三阶段渐进训练策略提升空间感知同时保持语义推理能力。
AI.result: 在nuScenes开环评估和Bench2Drive闭环评估中达到最先进性能。在3D检测、在线映射、运动预测和驾驶导向VQA等多种感知、预测和理解任务上展示强性能，突显其作为自动驾驶统一模型的广泛适用性。
AI.conclusion: UniDriveVLA通过专家解耦策略成功解决了VLA模型在自动驾驶中的感知-推理冲突问题，实现了空间感知与语义推理的有效平衡。作为统一模型，它在多项任务中展现卓越性能，为自动驾驶系统的认知能力提升提供了新思路。

relevance_reason: This paper is about autonomous driving (vehicle perception, scene understanding, action planning for self-driving vehicles), which is completely different from 3D Human Motion Generation in Computer Graphics. While both may use Vision-Language Models, the application domain is entirely different - this paper focuses on vehicle 3D detection, motion prediction, and driving action planning for autonomous vehicles, not on generating human motion from 2D videos.

title: SCALE: Semantic- and Confidence-Aware Conditional Variational Autoencoder for Zero-shot Skeleton-based Action Recognition
summary: Zero-shot skeleton-based action recognition (ZSAR) aims to recognize action classes without any training skeletons from those classes, relying instead on auxiliary semantics from text. Existing approaches frequently depend on explicit skeleton-text alignment, which can be brittle when action names underspecify fine-grained dynamics and when unseen classes are semantically confusable. We propose SCALE, a lightweight and deterministic Semantic- and Confidence-Aware Listwise Energy-based framework that formulates ZSAR as class-conditional energy ranking. SCALE builds a text-conditioned Conditional Variational Autoencoder where frozen text representations parameterize both the latent prior and the decoder, enabling likelihood-based evaluation for unseen classes without generating samples at test time. To separate competing hypotheses, we introduce a semantic- and confidence-aware listwise energy loss that emphasizes semantically similar hard negatives and incorporates posterior uncertainty to adapt decision margins and reweight ambiguous training instances. Additionally, we utilize a latent prototype contrast objective to align posterior means with text-derived latent prototypes, improving semantic organization and class separability without direct feature matching. Experiments on NTU-60 and NTU-120 datasets show that SCALE consistently improves over prior VAE- and alignment-based baselines while remaining competitive with diffusion-based methods.

AI.tldr: SCALE是一种基于语义和置信度感知列表能量的零样本骨架动作识别框架，通过文本条件变分自编码器进行类别条件能量排序，无需为未见类别生成样本即可进行似然评估。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖于显式的骨架-文本对齐，当动作名称无法详细描述细粒度动态且未见类别在语义上难以区分时，这种方法变得脆弱。
AI.method: 提出文本条件条件变分自编码器(CVAE)，冻结的文本表示参数化潜在先验和解码器；引入语义-置信度感知的列表能量损失，强调语义相似的困难负样本并结合后验不确定性来调整决策边界和重加权模糊训练实例；此外利用潜在原型对比目标将后验均值与文本衍生的潜在原型对齐。
AI.result: 在NTU-60和NTU-120数据集上的实验表明，SCALE始终优于先前基于VAE和对齐的方法，同时与基于扩散的方法保持竞争力。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过能量排序和语义感知成功提升了零样本动作识别的性能。

relevance_reason: This paper is about skeleton-based ACTION RECOGNITION (classification task), not 3D MOTION GENERATION. The research profile specifically needs video-to-motion translation methods, but this paper works on classifying pre-existing skeleton sequences. It uses CVAE for zero-shot recognition, which is fundamentally different from generating new motion from video inputs. The methods, application domain, and goals are entirely different - recognition vs generation. No mention of video generation models, VLMs, or any visual-to-motion pipeline.

title: SPAR: Single-Pass Any-Resolution ViT for Open-vocabulary Segmentation
summary: Foundational Vision Transformers (ViTs) have limited effectiveness in tasks requiring fine-grained spatial understanding, due to their fixed pre-training resolution and inherently coarse patch-level representations. These challenges are especially pronounced in dense prediction scenarios, such as open-vocabulary segmentation with ViT-based vision-language models, where high-resolution inputs are essential for accurate pixel-level reasoning. Existing approaches typically process large-resolution images using a sliding-window strategy at the pre-training resolution. While this improves accuracy through finer strides, it comes at a significant computational cost. We introduce SPAR: Single-Pass Any-Resolution ViT, a resolution-agnostic dense feature extractor designed for efficient high-resolution inference. We distill the spatial reasoning capabilities of a finely-strided, sliding-window teacher into a single-pass student using a feature regression loss, without requiring architectural changes or pixel-level supervision. Applied to open-vocabulary segmentation, SPAR improves single-pass baselines by up to 10.5 mIoU and even surpasses the teacher, demonstrating effectiveness in efficient, high-resolution reasoning. Code: https://github.com/naomikombol/SPAR

AI.tldr: SPAR是一种用于ViT的高效高分辨率推理的特征提取器，通过知识蒸馏将滑动窗口教师的细粒度空间推理能力迁移到单次通学生模型，无需架构改变或像素级监督，在开放词汇分割任务中显著提升性能。
AI.motivation: 基础Vision Transformers因固定预训练分辨率和粗粒度的patch级表示，在需要细粒度空间理解的任务中效果有限。现有方法处理高分辨率图像采用滑动窗口策略，虽能提升精度但计算成本过高。
AI.method: 提出SPAR (Single-Pass Any-Resolution ViT)，一种分辨率无关的密集特征提取器。通过特征回归损失将细粒度步长的滑动窗口教师的空间推理能力蒸馏到单次通学生，无需架构更改或像素级监督。
AI.result: 在开放词汇分割任务中，SPAR将单次通基线提升最多10.5 mIoU，甚至超越教师模型，展现出高效高分辨率推理的有效性。
AI.conclusion: SPAR通过知识蒸馏成功实现了高效、高分辨率的ViT推理，在保持计算效率的同时显著提升了细粒度空间理解能力，为密集预测任务提供了一种实用的解决方案。

relevance_reason: This paper is about open-vocabulary image segmentation using Vision Transformers, which is completely unrelated to 3D Human Motion Generation. While both involve feature extraction, the application domains are entirely different - one is about segmenting static images, the other is about generating 3D human motion from video inputs. The paper does not address any of the pain points mentioned (domain gap between 2D video and 3D motion, temporal consistency, quality control, visual guidance features for motion generation). No temporal modeling, motion generation, or video-to-motion translation methods are involved.

title: Modular Energy Steering for Safe Text-to-Image Generation with Foundation Models
summary: Controlling the behavior of text-to-image generative models is critical for safe and practical deployment. Existing safety approaches typically rely on model fine-tuning or curated datasets, which can degrade generation quality or limit scalability. We propose an inference-time steering framework that leverages gradient feedback from frozen pretrained foundation models to guide the generation process without modifying the underlying generator. Our key observation is that vision-language foundation models encode rich semantic representations that can be repurposed as off-the-shelf supervisory signals during generation. By injecting such feedback through clean latent estimates at each sampling step, our method formulates safety steering as an energy-based sampling problem. This design enables modular, training-free safety control that is compatible with both diffusion and flow-matching models and can generalize across diverse visual concepts. Experiments demonstrate state-of-the-art robustness against NSFW red-teaming benchmarks and effective multi-target steering, while preserving high generation quality on benign non-targeted prompts. Our framework provides a principled approach for utilizing foundation models as semantic energy estimators, enabling reliable and scalable safety control for text-to-image generation.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种无需训练的推理时安全 steering 框架，利用视觉-语言基础模型的梯度反馈来引导文本到图像生成过程，在不修改生成器的情况下实现模块化、可扩展的安全控制。
AI.motivation: 现有安全方法通常依赖模型微调或精选数据集，这可能会降低生成质量或限制可扩展性，需要一种在不损害生成质量的前提下提供安全控制的方法。
AI.method: 提出推理时 steering 框架，利用冻结的预训练视觉-语言基础模型编码的丰富语义表示作为现成的监督信号。通过在每个采样步骤通过干净的潜在估计注入此类反馈，将安全 steering 表述为基于能量的采样问题。
AI.result: 在 NSFW 红队基准测试中实现了最先进的鲁棒性，有效的多目标 steering，同时在良性非目标提示上保持高生成质量。
AI.conclusion: 为利用基础模型作为语义能量估计器提供了原则性方法，实现了文本到图像生成的可靠和可扩展安全控制。

relevance_reason: This paper is about text-to-image generation (2D image synthesis) for safety control, which is completely different from the research field of 3D Human Motion Generation. While both use Visual Language Models (VLMs), the application domains are entirely different: image generation vs motion generation. The paper addresses safety/NSFW filtering in 2D image generation, while the research profile focuses on domain gap between 2D video and 3D motion, temporal consistency in visual-to-motion translation, and quality control of generated video content. This is a clear case of using similar methods (VLMs) but in a completely wrong application domain.

title: AdamFlow: Adam-based Wasserstein Gradient Flows for Surface Registration in Medical Imaging
summary: Surface registration plays an important role for anatomical shape analysis in medical imaging. Existing surface registration methods often face a trade-off between efficiency and robustness. Local point matching methods are computationally efficient, but vulnerable to noise and initialisation. Methods designed for global point set alignment tend to incur a high computational cost. To address the challenge, here we present a fast surface registration method, which formulates surface meshes as probability measures and surface registration as a distributional optimisation problem. The discrepancy between two meshes is measured using an efficient sliced Wasserstein distance with log-linear computational complexity. We propose a novel optimisation method, AdamFlow, which generalises the well-known Adam optimisation method from the Euclidean space to the probability space for minimising the sliced Wasserstein distance. We theoretically analyse the asymptotic convergence of AdamFlow and empirically demonstrate its superior performance in both affine and non-rigid surface registration across various anatomical structures.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种名为AdamFlow的快速表面注册方法，将表面网格表示为概率测度，通过sliced Wasserstein距离进行分布优化，在保证鲁棒性的同时实现了高效计算。
AI.motivation: 现有表面注册方法在效率与鲁棒性之间存在权衡：局部点匹配方法计算高效但对噪声和初始化敏感；全局点集对齐方法鲁棒但计算成本高。
AI.method: 将表面网格形式化为概率测度，将表面注册问题转化为分布优化问题。使用计算复杂度为对数线性的高效sliced Wasserstein距离度量两个网格间的差异。提出AdamFlow优化方法，将欧几里得空间中的Adam优化器推广到概率空间以最小化sliced Wasserstein距离。
AI.result: 在多种解剖结构的仿射和非刚性表面注册任务上验证了该方法的优越性能。
AI.conclusion: AdamFlow在保持高计算效率的同时，提高了表面注册的鲁棒性，解决了传统方法在效率与鲁棒性之间的权衡问题。理论分析证明了该方法的渐近收敛性。

relevance_reason: This paper is about medical imaging surface registration (mesh-to-mesh alignment) using optimal transport and Wasserstein gradient flows. The application domain is completely different from 3D human motion generation - it deals with anatomical surface matching in medical imaging, not human motion synthesis from video. The method (probability measures + sliced Wasserstein distance) is also not relevant to visual-to-motion translation, video generation, or VLM-based quality supervision needed for the research profile.

title: A Simple Baseline for Streaming Video Understanding
summary: Recent streaming video understanding methods increasingly rely on complex memory mechanisms to handle long video streams. We challenge this trend with a simple finding: a sliding-window baseline that feeds only the most recent N frames to an off-the-shelf VLM already matches or surpasses published streaming models. We formalize this baseline as SimpleStream and evaluate it against 13 major offline and online video LLM baselines on OVO-Bench and StreamingBench. Despite its simplicity, SimpleStream delivers consistently strong performance. With only 4 recent frames, it reaches 67.7% average accuracy on OVO-Bench and 80.59% on StreamingBench. Controlled ablations further show that the value of longer context is backbone-dependent rather than uniformly increasing with model scale, and reveal a consistent perception-memory trade-off: adding more historical context can improve recall, but often weakens real-time perception. This suggests that stronger memory, retrieval, or compression modules should not be taken as evidence of progress unless they clearly outperform SimpleStream under the same protocol. We therefore argue that future streaming benchmarks should separate recent-scene perception from long-range memory, so that performance improvements from added complexity can be evaluated more clearly.

AI.tldr: SimpleStream通过仅使用最近4帧的滑动窗口方法，在两个流式视频理解基准上匹配或超越13个复杂记忆机制模型，揭示了复杂记忆模块可能被高估，当前流式视频理解进展可能被夸大。
AI.motivation: 挑战流式视频理解领域日益依赖复杂记忆机制的趋势，揭示简单滑动窗口基线的竞争力。
AI.method: 将SimpleStream形式化为滑动窗口基线，仅将最近N帧输入现成的视频大模型(VLM)。在OVO-Bench和StreamingBench上与13个主流离线/在线视频LLM基线进行对比评估。
AI.result: 仅使用4帧，SimpleStream在OVO-Bench达到67.7%平均准确率，在StreamingBench达到80.59%，优于或匹配多数已发布的流式模型。消融实验表明：更长上下文的价值取决于骨干模型而非随模型规模单调增加；存在一致的感知-记忆权衡——增加历史上下文可提升召回但往往削弱实时感知。
AI.conclusion: 未来流式视频基准测试应分离近景感知与长程记忆，以便更清晰地评估复杂性带来的性能提升。除非明确超越SimpleStream，否则不应将更强的记忆、检索或压缩模块视为进步证据。

relevance_reason: The paper is about "Streaming Video Understanding" - using a sliding window approach with VLMs to understand video content. This is completely different from Computer Graphics - 3D Human Motion Generation. The paper doesn't address 3D motion generation, visual-to-motion translation, or any domain related to creating human motion from video. Despite both using VLMs (a method overlap), the application domains are entirely different - video understanding vs. 3D motion generation are unrelated fields.

title: Large-scale Codec Avatars: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Large-scale Avatar Pretraining
summary: High-quality 3D avatar modeling faces a critical trade-off between fidelity and generalization. On the one hand, multi-view studio data enables high-fidelity modeling of humans with precise control over expressions and poses, but it struggles to generalize to real-world data due to limited scale and the domain gap between the studio environment and the real world. On the other hand, recent large-scale avatar models trained on millions of in-the-wild samples show promise for generalization across a wide range of identities, yet the resulting avatars are often of low-quality due to inherent 3D ambiguities. To address this, we present Large-Scale Codec Avatars (LCA), a high-fidelity, full-body 3D avatar model that generalizes to world-scale populations in a feedforward manner, enabling efficient inference. Inspired by the success of large language models and vision foundation models, we present, for the first time, a pre/post-training paradigm for 3D avatar modeling at scale: we pretrain on 1M in-the-wild videos to learn broad priors over appearance and geometry, then post-train on high-quality curated data to enhance expressivity and fidelity. LCA generalizes across hair styles, clothing, and demographics while providing precise, fine-grained facial expressions and finger-level articulation control, with strong identity preservation. Notably, we observe emergent generalization to relightability and loose garment support to unconstrained inputs, and zero-shot robustness to stylized imagery, despite the absence of direct supervision.

AI.tldr: 提出了Large-Scale Codec Avatars (LCA)，一种高保真、全身3D头像模型，通过预训练/后训练范式在100万野外视频上进行预训练，然后在高质量精选数据上进行后训练，实现了在世界范围内人群的泛化，同时保持精确的面部表情和手指关节控制。
AI.motivation: 3D头像建模面临保真度与泛化性之间的关键权衡：多视角工作室数据虽能实现高保真建模但难以泛化到真实世界；而大规模头像模型虽能泛化但质量较低。为解决这一矛盾，需要一种既能保持高保真又能泛化到世界范围人群的方法。
AI.method: 提出首个大规模3D头像建模的预训练/后训练范式：1)在大规模野外视频(100万)上预训练以学习外观和几何的广泛先验；2)在高质量精选数据上后训练以增强表达力和保真度。该模型支持精确的细粒度面部表情和手指级关节控制。
AI.result: LCA能够跨发型、服装和人口统计学特征泛化，同时提供精确的细粒度面部表情和手指级关节控制，具有强身份保持能力。值得注意的是，尽管没有直接监督，但观察到对光照可重Lighting性和宽松衣物支持的 emergent 泛化，以及对风格化图像的零样本鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: LCA是首个高保真、全身3D头像模型，能够以 feedforward 方式泛化到世界范围内的人群，实现高效推理。首次将大语言模型和视觉基础模型的成功范式应用于3D头像建模，为大规模头像建模确立了新的范式。

relevance_reason: This paper is about 3D Avatar modeling (creating high-fidelity digital human representations/avatars), not 3D Human Motion Generation. While both involve human representation, they are distinct application domains: Avatar modeling focuses on identity preservation, facial expressions, and finger control for digital doubles - not on generating human motions/actions. The pain points are completely different: LCA addresses avatar fidelity vs. generalization tradeoff, whereas the research profile addresses temporal consistency in video-to-motion translation and extracting visual guidance features. No overlap in methods or goals.

title: Beyond Referring Expressions: Scenario Comprehension Visual Grounding
summary: Existing visual grounding benchmarks primarily evaluate alignment between image regions and literal referring expressions, where models can often succeed by matching a prominent named category. We explore a complementary and more challenging setting of scenario-based visual grounding, where the target must be inferred from roles, intentions, and relational context rather than explicit naming. We introduce Referring Scenario Comprehension (RSC), a benchmark designed for this setting. The queries in this benchmark are paragraph-length texts that describe object roles, user goals, and contextual cues, including deliberate references to distractor objects that often require deep understanding to resolve. Each instance is annotated with interpretable difficulty tags for uniqueness, clutter, size, overlap, and position which expose distinct failure modes and support fine-grained analysis. RSC contains approximately 31k training examples, 4k in-domain test examples, and a 3k out-of-distribution split with unseen object categories. We further propose ScenGround, a curriculum reasoning method serving as a reference point for this setting, combining supervised warm-starting with difficulty-aware reinforcement learning. Experiments show that scenario-based queries expose systematic failures in current models that standard benchmarks do not reveal, and that curriculum training improves performance on challenging slices and transfers to standard benchmarks.

AI.tldr: 本文提出RSC（场景理解指代）基准，用于评估基于场景描述而非显式命名的视觉指代任务，包含约31k训练样本和7k测试样本，并提出ScenGround Curriculum推理方法。
AI.motivation: 现有视觉指代基准主要评估图像区域与字面指代表达的对应关系，模型可通过匹配显式命名类别成功。本文探索更具挑战性的场景化视觉指代设置，需要从角色、意图和关系上下文推断目标。
AI.method: 提出ScenGround方法，结合监督预热和难度感知强化学习的课程推理策略。RSC基准包含约31k训练样本、4k域内测试样本和3k分布外样本，每个实例标注了独特性、混乱度、大小、重叠和位置等难度标签。
AI.result: 实验表明场景化查询暴露了当前模型在标准基准上未揭示的系统性失败，课程训练提升了困难样本的性能并可迁移到标准基准。
AI.conclusion: 场景化视觉指代是一个重要的新研究方向，RSC基准和ScenGround方法为该领域提供了有效的评估和训练框架。

relevance_reason: This paper is about Visual Grounding in Computer Vision - understanding scene context to locate objects based on textual descriptions. The application domain is completely different from 3D Human Motion Generation. While both mention VLMs, this paper uses VLMs for visual grounding/understanding tasks (identifying objects in images), not for motion generation or quality control in video-to-motion translation. The paper doesn't address any of the stated pain points (domain gap, temporal consistency, quality control, visual guidance extraction for motion generation).

title: Modulate-and-Map: Crossmodal Feature Mapping with Cross-View Modulation for 3D Anomaly Detection
summary: We present ModMap, a natively multiview and multimodal framework for 3D anomaly detection and segmentation. Unlike existing methods that process views independently, our method draws inspiration from the crossmodal feature mapping paradigm to learn to map features across both modalities and views, while explicitly modelling view-dependent relationships through feature-wise modulation. We introduce a cross-view training strategy that leverages all possible view combinations, enabling effective anomaly scoring through multiview ensembling and aggregation. To process high-resolution 3D data, we train and publicly release a foundational depth encoder tailored to industrial datasets. Experiments on SiM3D, a recent benchmark that introduces the first multiview and multimodal setup for 3D anomaly detection and segmentation, demonstrate that ModMap attains state-of-the-art performance by surpassing previous methods by wide margins.

AI.tldr: ModMap是一个原生支持多视图和多模态的3D异常检测与分割框架，通过跨模态特征映射和特征级调制技术学习跨模态和跨视图的特征表示，并采用跨视图训练策略实现多视图集成，在SiM3D基准测试上达到最先进水平。
AI.motivation: 现有方法独立处理各个视图，缺乏跨模态和跨视图的特征学习能力，无法有效建模视图依赖关系。
AI.method: 提出跨模态特征映射范式，学习在模态和视图之间映射特征，并通过特征级调制显式建模视图依赖关系。引入跨视图训练策略，利用所有可能的视图组合，通过多视图集成和聚合实现有效的异常评分。同时训练并公开发布了针对工业数据集优化的基础深度编码器，用于处理高分辨率3D数据。
AI.result: 在SiM3D基准测试（首个多视图多模态3D异常检测和分割设置）上取得了最先进性能，以较大优势超越之前的方法。
AI.conclusion: ModMap通过在SiM3D基准测试中取得最先进性能，证明了多视图和多模态方法在3D异常检测和分割中的有效性。该方法为工业3D异常检测提供了一种新的思路。

relevance_reason: This paper is about 3D anomaly detection for industrial datasets (SiM3D benchmark), not 3D human motion generation. Although it deals with 3D data and cross-view/cross-modal features, the application domain is completely different - industrial quality inspection vs. human motion generation from video. The pain points, methods used in practice, and end goals are entirely different.

title: Generative World Renderer
summary: Scaling generative inverse and forward rendering to real-world scenarios is bottlenecked by the limited realism and temporal coherence of existing synthetic datasets. To bridge this persistent domain gap, we introduce a large-scale, dynamic dataset curated from visually complex AAA games. Using a novel dual-screen stitched capture method, we extracted 4M continuous frames (720p/30 FPS) of synchronized RGB and five G-buffer channels across diverse scenes, visual effects, and environments, including adverse weather and motion-blur variants. This dataset uniquely advances bidirectional rendering: enabling robust in-the-wild geometry and material decomposition, and facilitating high-fidelity G-buffer-guided video generation. Furthermore, to evaluate the real-world performance of inverse rendering without ground truth, we propose a novel VLM-based assessment protocol measuring semantic, spatial, and temporal consistency. Experiments demonstrate that inverse renderers fine-tuned on our data achieve superior cross-dataset generalization and controllable generation, while our VLM evaluation strongly correlates with human judgment. Combined with our toolkit, our forward renderer enables users to edit styles of AAA games from G-buffers using text prompts.

AI.tldr: 本文构建了一个来自AAA游戏的大规模动态数据集（4M帧，RGB+五通道G-buffer），提出双屏拼接捕获方法和VLM评估协议，用于推进双向渲染和G-buffer引导的视频生成。
AI.motivation: 现有合成数据集的有限真实性和时间一致性限制了生成式逆渲染和前向渲染在真实场景中的扩展，存在显著的领域差距。
AI.method: 采用新型双屏拼接捕获方法，从AAA游戏中提取4M连续帧（720p/30 FPS），包含同步RGB和五个G-buffer通道；提出基于VLM的评估协议，测量语义、空间和时间一致性。
AI.result: 在数据集上微调的逆渲染器实现了更好的跨数据集泛化和可控生成；VLM评估与人类判断高度相关；工具包支持从G-buffer使用文本提示编辑AAA游戏风格。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集和评估协议能显著提升逆渲染的跨数据集泛化能力和可控生成质量，VLM评估与人类判断高度相关，前向渲染器支持文本提示编辑AAA游戏风格。

relevance_reason: This paper is about G-buffer guided video generation from AAA games (rendering/video synthesis domain), NOT about 3D human motion generation. While it uses VLM for evaluation and addresses temporal consistency, these are applied to a completely different application domain. The paper focuses on inverse rendering, forward rendering, and video generation from game engine data - not on extracting and generating human motion from 2D videos. The application domain mismatch is fundamental - this is about graphics rendering, not motion generation.
